pages.text: The Sky Configurator stores a line ID and resource toggles. Right-click air to open the configurator screen; right-click a node to open its node screen. Holding the configurator in the offhand while placing a node makes the new node inherit its line and resource toggles.
The configurator is the starting point for creating lines and copying settings in bulk.

Sneak-right-click a node with the configurator to copy that node and enter paste mode. While in paste mode, right-click another node to write the stored line and resource toggles to it. Sneak-right-click again, open the configurator screen, or stop holding the configurator to leave paste mode.

Speed upgrades go into node upgrade slots. Without one, a node processes 1 item slot or fluid tank per tick; with one, it processes 2 slots or tanks per tick. This improves the node's slot/tank throughput, but it does not add a hidden line buffer.

Dimension upgrades also go into node upgrade slots, but only affect extract faces. An extract face with a dimension upgrade can send to same-line insert faces in other loaded dimensions. Insert faces do not need dimension upgrades. This is not a chunk loader; unloaded dimensions or chunks are skipped.
The dimension upgrade requires a Nether Star. It only needs to be installed on the extracting node.
